definition: CMP_MAP gradient accumulates partial-match feedback across many sequential single-byte or string comparisons in a multi-step grammar or state machine, driving corpus depth far beyond what edge-only feedback reaches.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards intermediate parsing progress through chained upstream string comparisons, driving corpus 10x-70x deeper into the XML parsing pipeline than cmplog's unguided I2S substitution reaches.
signature.one_line: VP gradient climbs multi-step XML parse chain to reach error enum arm

analysis.summary_one_line: XML input that drives the parser to fire xmlFatalErr with the integer enum value XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF (a malformed &#x... hex character reference inside the parsed document).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML document body that exercises the entity/character-reference parsing path deeply enough to invoke xmlFatalErr with the specific enum constant XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF — i.e. an input containing structures like '<?xml' declarations, DTDs, or entity references that lead the parser to scan a malformed hex character reference. The blocking branch is the integer-switch arm 'case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF:' on the runtime value of the parameter 'error' passed by an upstream caller; the input must drive a caller path that passes that exact enum value.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that bring intermediate-stage CMP operands (e.g. the byte-by-byte comparisons inside xmlIsNameChar, xmlParseNCName, xmlParseStartTag2, xmlParseCharData, xmlParseCharDataComplex) closer to their target tokens, letting the corpus climb the multi-step XML parsing pipeline and reach a caller that fires XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF. The HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE table shows W-side invocations of these upstream parsing functions dominate L by 10x–70x (e.g. xmlIsNameChar 560 vs 0; xmlParseNCName 401 vs 29), confirming the winner navigates deeper into the parser. value_profile_cmplog resolved 10/10 trials with avg duration 4.65h.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has input-to-state substitution (which can substitute the integer constant XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF directly into the input where its bytes appear in a CMP buffer) but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient. Without the gradient feedback on the many intermediate string/byte comparisons in the upstream parsing chain, cmplog only stochastically navigates to the caller that produces this specific error enum — it resolved only 1/10 trials (in trial 9, after 426s of mutations), while value_profile_cmplog resolved every trial.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient. The technique difference between winner (value_profile_cmplog) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile — i.e., the CMP_MAP/Hamming-similarity feedback. Both fuzzers carry input-to-state substitution, so the integer switch-case substitution capability is shared; what cmplog lacks is the upstream gradient signal that rewards inputs incrementally approaching deeper parsing states. The 10x–70x W/L hit-count divergence on upstream NameChar/NCName/StartTag2/CharData functions is the direct fingerprint of CMP_MAP guiding corpus growth along the parsing chain that culminates in firing XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a switch arm 'case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF:' inside xmlFatalErr, gating on the integer 'error' parameter.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog > cmplog with delta=value_profile, where vpc resolved 10/10 and cmplog resolved 1/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
--- Pair 1: value_profile_cmplog > cmplog  [delta: value_profile] ---

  winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: On the branch line itself, winner-side seeds take the true side 17 times across 71 invocations of xmlFatalErr; loser-side seeds take it 0 times across 27 invocations.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 256  T=17 F=54  T=0 F=27  case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog's instrumentation adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's I2S substitution; both fuzzers share I2SRandReplace, so the technique difference between them is exactly the value_profile feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

**Feedback**: edge-bucket + CMP_MAP-bucket signals.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's mutator stack is naive's havoc+token plus I2SRandReplace; it has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so it cannot incrementally climb partial-match comparison chains.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that exposes the same integer switch-arm shape (multi-case switch on a runtime enum parameter, reachable only via a deep multi-step CMP chain whose intermediate comparisons can be tracked by CMP_MAP) where value_profile and value_profile_cmplog do NOT outperform naive and cmplog at long horizons (e.g. dose-response curves with overlapping medians at all chain depths). That would mean the CMP_MAP gradient confers no advantage at the upstream chain and the observed divergence at this branch must be due to some other factor.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The argument that CMP_MAP gradient (rather than some other byproduct of vpc's larger feedback bucket set) is what drives the 10x-70x upstream hit-count divergence is inferred from the technique-delta and the mechanism library paragraphs, not from a direct CMP_MAP bucket trace.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records per-character proximity buckets at every single-byte state-transition gate in the DOCTYPE state machine, building a gradient through the chained character comparisons that naive's edge-only counter cannot signal.
signature.one_line: VP gradient navigates chained single-byte state-machine gates toward bracket sequence

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must drive the DTD-scan state machine to state=']' by containing a complete '<!DOCTYPE ... [...]' internal-subset structure.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed DOCTYPE prologue followed by an internal-subset opener: the byte stream must traverse the xmlParseLookupInternalSubset state machine through state=0 -> state='"' (via *cur=='"' at line 11304) -> back to state=0 -> *cur=='[' (also at line 11304, sets state=']') so that the blocker `else if (state == ']')` evaluates true. Equivalently, the input must contain the literal sequence `<!DOCTYPE <name> ... "..." ... [` reaching the state-machine's bracket-tracking arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile reaches this branch on every trial (resolved=10/10) and the per-role overlay shows it executing lines 11269-11272 (state=0; start=cur; continue;) exclusively — meaning value_profile actually enters the state==']' arm, encounters a non-']' non-'>' non-blank byte, and continues the scan. At the L11262 sub-branch the winner has T=0 F=24, i.e. it entered the state==']' block 24 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 trials and is unreached on 2/10. Its hitcount on the blocker is 0. The overlay shows lines 11263-11265 (the L-only success-path of state==']' && *cur=='>') reached only by loser-blocking seeds — i.e. the few times the loser enters the block it immediately exits via return(1), while never producing the broader exploration that the winner does. naive lacks any CMP-distance signal so it cannot build a gradient through the chained single-byte character comparisons (*cur=='<', '"', '\'', ']', '>', etc.) that gate every state transition; without that gradient, it spends 6.31h on average blocked here vs the winner's 1.10h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a (PC, Hamming-distance-bucket) entry on every intercepted character-equality CMP. Each distinct byte at *cur produces a new bucket at PCs like L11262 (*cur=='>'), L11267 (IS_BLANK_CH), L11269 (*cur!=']'), L11293 (*cur=='<'), L11304 (*cur=='"' || '\'' || ']'). This per-byte feedback rewards inputs that drive each gate one step closer to the target character, building up the multi-step DOCTYPE/internal-subset grammar that naive cannot stumble onto with edge-only feedback. cmplog (I2S substitution) helps less here because each gate is a 1-byte literal comparison rather than a multi-byte magic — there is little to substitute, but the CMP_MAP buckets still reward proximity.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair at this branch is value_profile>naive on the value_profile technique axis (not I2S).
value_profile resolves every trial; naive resolves zero and is blocked 8/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            0        8          2  loser (value_profile vs value_profile)
cmplog                           2        8          0  REFERENCE
value_profile                   10        0          0  winner (value_profile vs naive)

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the chained-else-if test against the literal ']' character in the internal-subset scan state machine.

state is assigned the value ']' when the scan encounters a '[' or other quote/bracket character at line 11304 — i.e. the input must literally drive the state machine through a '[' (or quote) before this branch can fire.
The function comment explicitly describes the closing-token sequence the state machine is searching for: "']' S? '>'".
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Internal subset ends with "']' S? '>'" in an unescaped section and

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-only coverage of lines 11270-11272 inside the state==']' block proves the winner reaches and exercises the blocker's true arm, while loser-only coverage of 11263-11265 shows loser entering the block only via the immediate-exit path.
At line 11262 (*cur == '>'), winner enters the state==']' block 24 times vs loser's 4 times — a 6x divergence in how often the gate fires.
Winner spends 1.10h average on average blocked vs loser's 6.31h, and prob_div=1.00 confirms a clean per-branch separation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
avg duration blocked: winner=1.10h  loser=6.31h
  avg hitcount on branch: winner=12  loser=0
  prob_div=1.00  dur_div=5.21h  hit_div=12

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's instrumentation adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket) on top of naive's edge counters — direct per-byte gradient feedback at every character comparison.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's edge counters **plus** integer-CMP
interception, but instead of buffering operands per execution (cmplog),
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only edge-bucket signal from the SanitizerCoverage edge counters and has no CMP introspection of any kind.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates entry to its trap behind a chained-character-equality state machine over single-byte literals (like ']', '"', '[', '<', '>') would show naive >= value_profile resolution rate at every dose. If equivalently, removing the CMP_MAP feedback from value_profile (replacing with edge-only) leaves resolution rates indistinguishable from naive, then the CMP_MAP gradient is not the active mechanism.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The single-byte character CMPs in this state machine are the weakest case for the CMP_MAP-gradient story: a Hamming-distance bucket on a 1-byte operand only distinguishes 8 distance classes, providing less gradient than multi-byte comparisons would — yet the divergence here is among the cleanest in the dataset (prob_div=1.0). The structural-grammar-via-CMP_MAP story may be incomplete; there could be a secondary feedback path (e.g. CMP_MAP buckets on the multi-byte STARTUP comparisons like the L11553 (cur=='<') && (next=='?') chain) doing most of the work upstream.
